text: The Automatic Parking Assist (APA) system shall detect available parking spaces using ultrasonic sensors with a minimum detection range of 4.5 meters.

The driver shall continuously monitor the vehicle environment and be prepared to take over control immediately during APA operation. The system shall issue a visual and auditory warning if driver attention is lost.

The parking trajectory computation shall complete within 200 milliseconds of slot detection to comply with real-time control loop requirements. REQ-001 must be satisfied before this function activates.
The Remote Parking Assist (RPA) function shall conform to ISO 26262 ASIL-B requirements for all safety-relevant software components, including the collision avoidance module.
The 360-degree camera system (Camera360) shall provide surround-view coverage with a minimum resolution of 1280x720 pixels at 30 fps for obstacle detection during parking maneuvers.
The Summon function shall only operate when the vehicle speed is below 10 km/h. The system shall automatically disengage and apply the brakes if this threshold is exceeded.
The parking ECU shall implement redundant sensor fusion combining ultrasonic sensor and Camera360 data to achieve a pedestrian detection false-negative rate below 0.1% per operational hour.
The APA interface shall provide the driver with real-time visual feedback of the detected parking space boundary overlay on the Camera360 surround-view image. REQ-005 shall be satisfied first.
All parking functions shall maintain a maximum lateral positioning error of ±5 cm relative to the target parking slot centerline, measured under nominal environmental conditions.
The system shall log all sensor data, ECU states, and driver interactions during parking maneuvers with a timestamp accuracy of ±1 ms for post-hoc analysis and certification evidence.
